objective: C3A-CP7 implements the next Checkpoint 3A child checkpoint: an executable performance probe that runs the local product/API path and evaluates bounded local/mock runtime performance evidence for the controlled demo path. Positive claims: C3A-CP7-HARNESS-001 dispatches the performance probe as an implemented executable pytest probe with shell=False and bounded/redacted failure output while preserving CP1 API E2E, CP2 output-correctness, CP3 language-quality, CP4 media-artifacts, CP5 access/quota/retention, and CP6 security/observability probes; C3A-CP7-PERFORMANCE-001 creates approved synthetic local project knowledge through API behavior, ingests/chunks/stores it, generates grounded walkthrough output, and records API-visible local/mock timing evidence for the critical path with run/request IDs, operation names, elapsed durations, explicit thresholds, local/mock posture, pass/fail status, and redacted failure context; C3A-CP7-NEG-001 proves docs/prose/static-snapshot checks, style-only status text, canned-success files, success-shaped dicts without runtime nonce/source/run binding, missing run/request/performance binding, stale evidence, cross-project replay, raw unsafe content leaks, timeout/subprocess failures, and implicit thresholds inadequate. The later real-browser E2E probe remains planned and does not complete Checkpoint 3A. Repository-ledger cleanup for PR #266 and issue #265 may be bundled because this is a substantive child checkpoint rather than a standalone status-only follow-up.
